source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Device - 22 inch Casing Shoe Failure

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Keathley Canyon Block 147

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Casing Shoe Failure
event.incident_type: Pollution - Unauthorized discharge of drilling mud
actions_taken: Installed temporary deflector plate to prevent hydrates on SSBOP
Issued E-100 and G-110 Incidents of Noncompliance
event.summary: On 1 April 2015, the SeaDrill West Vela drill ship under contract to BP reported a dark area identified by ROV approximately 400-500 feet from the wellhead at Well 001 (Tiber #3) in Keathley Canyon Block 147. Samples from the seafloor showed gelatinous material resembling filter cake with 2% oil content, consistent with Synthetic Based Drilling Mud used during the 16.5 inch hole section drilling. The incident resulted from a 22 inch casing shoe failure at 8630 ft md/tvd set in a salt formation. From 18-22 January 2015, approximately 500 barrels of 14.4 ppg synthetic oil base mud were discharged into offshore waters during 14 inch intermediate casing running and cementing operations. Total mud losses during casing operations reached approximately 4802 barrels.
recommendations: Management should create a Condition of Approval (COA) by the District Operations Support (DOS) Section to place on all drilling permits requiring operators to contact the district before continuing operations when extreme losses are experienced during drilling and casing running operations in any hole section

threats.name: 22 inch casing shoe failure due to salt formation weakness
Formation breakdown below 22 inch casing during pressure testing
Excessive surge pressures from 14 inch casing running operations

consequences.name: Loss of containment - drilling mud discharge to seafloor
Environmental pollution - approximately 500 barrels of synthetic oil base mud released

controls.name: 22 inch casing pressure testing
controls.barrier_role: Verify casing integrity before proceeding with deeper operations
controls.lod_basis: Primary barrier to detect casing defects prior to operations

supporting_text: At 1930 hours on 2 January 2015, the 22 inch casing was pressure tested to 4500 psi for 30 minutes with 8.6 ppg seawater. Test met BSEE requirements, pumped 29 bbls during test and bled back 29 bbls at end of test
At 0000 hours on 3 January 2015, the 22 inch casing was pressure tested again per GMTBR-DO-PR-00-03204 to 4500 psi with 8.6 ppg seawater
A potential 22 inch casing connection leak during the sea water pressure test which could have contributed to the creation of micro-fractures, which then propagated into a micro-annulus

controls.name: Formation Integrity Test (FIT)
controls.barrier_role: Verify formation strength and casing shoe integrity before drilling deeper
controls.lod_basis: Primary barrier to assess formation capability to withstand drilling pressures

supporting_text: At 1100 hours a FIT was performed with 15.0 ppg SBM at 1,892 psi for an equivalent surface mud weight of 16.7 ppg (17.0 ppg down hole mud weight)
The probable cause of the incident was identified as the breakdown of the 22 inch casing shoe that was set at 8630 ft md/tvd in a salt formation

controls.name: Geologic and seismic analysis for well planning
controls.barrier_role: Identify geologic risks and inform well design decisions
controls.lod_basis: Pre-drilling assessment to prevent design inadequacies

supporting_text: The top of the salt formation was weaker than expected due to factors outlined in the Salt Fracture Initiation Line theory which discusses that salt strength assumptions in well planning processes may have underestimated potential salt strength failure modes
The team could not identify any down-hole data that would have changed the location and design of the well or prevented the event once it was in progress

controls.name: Mud loss monitoring and response procedures
controls.barrier_role: Detect and respond to excessive mud losses indicating formation breakdown
controls.lod_basis: Secondary barrier to detect operational anomalies and trigger corrective action

supporting_text: From January 18 to January 22, 2015, BP failed to prevent an unauthorized discharge of approximately 500 barrels of 14.4 pound per gallon synthetic oil base mud
While experiencing severe losses, BP failed to discontinue the 14 inch casing running operations to determine the cause of the losses (Total Loss was approximately 4802 bbls)
Total losses for 24 hour period 456 bbls, cumulative total loss 727 bbls
Losses for 12 hours were 550 bbls, cumulative total loss 1640 bbls
Losses for 12 hours were 1280 bbls, cumulative total loss 3912 bbls

controls.name: Operational supervision and well site leadership
controls.barrier_role: Ensure safe operations and decision-making during drilling activities
controls.lod_basis: Secondary barrier to ensure compliance with procedures and safe practices

supporting_text: An E-100 Incident of Noncompliance (INC) and G-110 INC were issued 'After the Fact' to document that BP Exploration and Production (BP) failed to provide adequate supervision during well operations on Well #001 (Tiber #3)
The G-110 INC was issued to BP for the failure to protect health, safety, property, and the environment by not performing all operations in a safe manner

controls.name: Subsea Blowout Preventer (SSBOP) installation and testing
controls.barrier_role: Prevent uncontrolled release of fluids in case of well integrity failure
controls.lod_basis: Final barrier to prevent catastrophic loss of containment

supporting_text: On 31 December 2014, the subsea blowout preventer (SSBOP) was run and latched onto the well head
On 1 January 2015, the testing of the SSBOP was completed

Notified BSEE Lake Charles District at 1000 hours on 16 January that during ROV inspection for methane sources, it was discovered that 3 of the fittings where the upper shut off valves attached to the low pressure wellhead housing have started to bubble

controls.name: Remote Operated Vehicle (ROV) inspection and monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Detect subsea anomalies and environmental releases
controls.lod_basis: Detection and monitoring of subsea conditions to identify problems

supporting_text: On 1 April 2015, the SeaDrill West Vela drill ship under contract to BP Exploration and Production (BP) reported a dark area identified by the rigs ROV approximately 400-500 feet from the well head located at Well 001 (Tiber #3)

Recent results of samples collected from the dark area on the seafloor show the material to be gelatinous, resemble filter cake, 2% oil based on retort analysis and consistent with Synthetic Based Drilling Mud (SBM)

controls.name: National Response Center (NRC) notification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure regulatory notification and environmental response coordination
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory compliance and environmental response activation

supporting_text: BP notified the National Response Center (NRC Report #1112477) and reported an unknown amount of SBM spill into the Gulf of Mexico

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
